title: alnus × hanedae
intro: Alnus × hanedae, also known as Haneda's alder, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between the Japanese alder (Alnus japonica) and the white alder (Alnus incana). The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Its health benefits include the potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and even have antimicrobial properties. Haneda's alder can be prepared as an herbal tea, decoction, or infusion, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine and herbal remedies.
